title: THETA Control Room — Build Hook Delta

changedProjects.lastKnownStatus: Build hook reported latest project state.
changedProjects.nextAction: v0.4.22 — PATINA PASS (user: meters look too clean, do a patina pass on the whole plugin). NEW namespace EP (Creosote Patina) in CreosoteTheme.h, placed above EU so EU::paintModuleShell can call it: reusable low-alpha grime/scuffs/dust/edgeWear/panel helpers, deterministic per-surface RNG (house pattern from EB::backlitCap), every layer clipped to the surface rounded-rect. APPLIED to every always-visible clean surface found by an audit of all 25: the McIntosh VU meters (yellowed haze blotches, water/heat stain, dust settled inside along the bottom glass, tarnished+worn-through frame line, faded patchy markings, fine scratches + cleaning swirls — all inside the CACHED renderFace so it costs nothing), the chassis background (biggest surface), the header bar (lighter 0.65 amount), module shells (seeded per module name so COMPRESS/GRIT/AMBER differ), LIMITER panel, METERING panel, and the BlankPlates. VERIFY IN ABLETON: VU meters read aged-but-still-beautiful McIntosh blue (not muddy); chassis/panels have subtle grime without muddying the lit controls; each module wears differently. DEFERRED (not visible on the default screen): TweakWindow chrome, OutputEq graph+knobs, AgeLfo scope+knobs, LicenseOverlay, ThetaLogo, header utility buttons, preset combo, bottom tabs — see the audit list. OPEN: painted-metal chassis fork.
